one_time_description: Subsequent to year-end, the First Nation agreed to purchase 50 acres of property for $23 million
economic_notes: Investments in multiple business partnerships including Shxw Kwimel Cha Limited Partnership (99%), Ts'elxweyeqw Tribe Limited Partnership (14%), and several management corporations. Tzeachten Investment Income of $2,200,041. Significant property tax and property transfer tax revenues indicating active land development. CMHC funding of $7,798,727 for housing.
notes: Property Taxation department is the only consolidated subsidiary. Two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Shxw Kwimel Cha Limited Partnership (99%) and Ts'elxweyeqw Tribe Limited Partnership (14%). Several other entities accounted for at cost. CMHC revenue of $7,798,727 is unusually large compared to prior year ($5,746) — likely a significant new housing program. Subsequent event: $23M land purchase agreed after year-end. Minors Trust Fund of $1,140,420 excluded from reporting entity. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $3,098,837 + Province of BC $442,225 + CMHC $7,798,727 + New Relationship Trust $336,000 + Sto:lo Nation $215,202 + FNLMRC $62,957 + First Nations Education Steering Committee $0 + Environment Canada $0 = $11,953,948; however CMHC is included in total_government_transfers. OSR calculated as total revenue minus all government transfers.
